Core Meaning: The rate at which a project consumes its budget or resources over a given period.
Expansion: Burn rate describes how quickly funding or effort is being used up. Teams watch it to judge whether spending pace is sustainable against the remaining budget and timeline.
Contrast: Burndown chart — burn rate is the pace of consumption; a burndown chart plots remaining work over time.
Example: A team spending 40,000 of a 200,000 budget each month has a burn rate that would exhaust funding in five months.
Source: not a formal PMI glossary entry

Core Meaning: A rhythm of activities conducted throughout a project, such as a fixed iteration or release frequency.
Expansion: Cadence is the regular, predictable rhythm at which a team performs recurring events such as iterations, reviews, or releases. A steady cadence creates reliable points for planning, feedback, and delivery. In adaptive delivery it supports flow and helps stakeholders anticipate when work and decisions occur.
Contrast: Iteration — cadence is the recurring rhythm of events; an iteration is a single timeboxed cycle within that rhythm.
Example: A team adopts a two-week cadence, holding planning at the start and a review and retrospective at the end of every iteration.
